entries.problem: A new SA member's account was not fully loaded in Legacy Admin, preventing an agent from sending a password reset link to the member.
entries.solution: An admin investigated and resolved the account data issue on the backend, after which a password reset link could be sent to the member via the Legacy Admin member page.

entries.problem: A member (UID 2069311732) submitted a GDPR/DSAR request that triggered partial account deletion, leaving them in a half-deleted state (removed from VSDB02 but not fully processed across all systems). They were unable to log in despite having an active Stock Advisor subscription, and did not intend for their account to be deleted.
entries.solution: Stop the ongoing DSAR deletion process. Rebuild the account by removing the old anonymized registration record and re-registering the user, which should pull the same Account ID and UID from the existing email address record still present in the email address API (e-cap). The email record still exists and Backoffice/Stripe retain their subscription data, so re-registration should restore the account without full data loss.

rawMessages.text:
Hi team! I have a member with an active Stock Advisor subscription in Backoffice, but there is no information listed in Legacy Admin. The login log shows they are blocked and the member is not able to log-in, which is why they reached out to MS. Could someone take a look and see if things can be fixed? Thanks!
Member info:
*Gareth Haigh*
<mailto:ghaigh1988@gmail.com|ghaigh1988@gmail.com>
UID 2069311732

It looks like this user must have submitted a GDPR request at some point and so were deleted from our systems. I am not sure why they are in back office, I think they are working on getting that all GDPR compliant. But that is why they cannot log in. Did they recently make this new stock advisor purchase on the same email they used before? <@U0743LN5XAQ> do you have any insight on the state of this user (exist in stripe/backoffice, email still associated with UID in email address /e-cap but not registration (anonymized in user API)
I think we might have a gap (or HAD a gap) that this user got through to not have their account fully reset when they GDPRd.... I can fix I think if they want to keep using that same email address.

If they submit a DSAR we block them so this happens. Tell them they filed a DSAR request that will delete all their data. 

If they want to stop the deletion process, we can go ahead and cancel the deletion. 

Otherwise, currently they are en route to get deleted  

I haven’t checked yet, but if they’re request is older than 10 days, they’ve likely already been deleted out of VSDB02

Oh this is interesting but may be a different strand to worry about, but it sounds like there is still a disconnect in the email address api vs user api for DSARs -- email address api still gets you an existing uid, e.g.
`<https://api-staging.fool.com/email-addresses/v1/ghaigh1988@gmail.com>`  -> ... `"Uid": 2069311732,"AccountId": "5effa71a-e0b0-401a-82ba-599e0714d7d0",`
so the account ID is still associated with the original email, but only in email address api (e-cap) we maybe need to nuke that as well on DSAR, right? I am assuming that that UID got pulled for the email on the latest purchase

So it depends where they are on in the flow. 

I'm currently waiting for VSDB02 to process and once it does I'll process the rest (auth0, hubspot, braze, etc). 

Stripe is currently not being DSARd and I know a couple locations that pulls from stripe that is re-injecting user data. Working on that though with Michal
so this user was not completely zapped before now?
I'll check tomorrow morning when I'm back to see where everything is at and get back here

just wondering if you did anything on this user since this thread started because I thought I was getting back an anonymized user when searching by UID on user api, but now I get 404. I might be mistaken on what I was seeing before so just wanted to verify if something might have changed
I have not. There is likely a VSDB02 script running now from <@U055Z0SEYMN>
Also, sometimes there are replications from VSDBO2 that take a while. So potentially it was deleted there and just didn’t propagate yet.
so for the root issue, i think we need to just completely delete their account and re-create. and this assumes I guess that they didn't really mean it with the DSAR?
I can try to do the re-registering, if that seems like the right appraoch
We should wait and see if they want to be deleted and go from there. <@W01AMQDTXL5> let us know if they want to proceed

This particular user is likely half deleted.

Their account is probably deleted from VSDB02 but I have not finished their DSAR. They submitted the request 50 days ago. Weird they are just messaging us now.

Confirm with them if they want it deleted still, if so we continue, if not we need to rebuild their account.
I could not find any email communication in Hubspot, so I did not know they were in the middle of a data deletion. I'll follow up once I hear back from the member. Thank you both!!
Member responded that he was not aware it would delete his account. Please stop the deletion and rebuild the members account.
Understood! We need to loop in <@U055Z0SEYMN> I believe to assist with this.
for the last list processed, the first two steps were already processed, the third step that changes their email is still pending due to an error, I don't have any scripts for undoing a deletion

Understood.

I don't know who to bug anymore for issues like this. I used to bug Manaen - do you have any idea how we can rebuild a new account for them?
unfortunately I was never involved in that
I'm curious if we could restore his information from a PartyDB backup, so we have him in our database, and then all he'd need to do is reset his password to log in.
remember this GDPR process does not delete anything
it applies "magic" to make the person disappear
lol yeah
unfortunately, it does not prevent them from still reaching out and bothering us
I have a feature request for you
Total Legacy Member Block - working on it
so I think its a matter of finding the person in PartyDB by alternate means and then fixing their record, I assume this is what Manaen would do

the email record still exists. I will double check but I am pretty sure we just need to remove the old registration record and then re-register, it will pull the same account ID and UID from the email record.
Backoffice has their data for sure
It pulls from stripe

entries.problem: A member purchased Stock Advisor and received a confirmation email, but their account showed as 'Not Registered' in Admin and did not exist in Auth0. The registration process failed to complete during purchase, preventing password reset options from functioning.
entries.solution: The user was manually registered in Auth0 by the identity services team. The member was then directed to reset their password via the reset password link on the login page, or alternatively a password reset link can be generated through the Admin UI.

entries.problem: Users receiving 'link already expired' errors when attempting to generate password reset links via Admin UI. The issue was intermittent — sometimes the link generation succeeded, sometimes it failed with a connection error. Affected users also sometimes had duplicate accounts in Auth0.
entries.solution: The intermittent errors were partly attributed to Auth0 instability that was being investigated and reportedly resolved. A workaround was to retry the password reset link generation multiple times, or to manually search the member in Legacy Admin and navigate to Auth0 from there. In at least one case, the issue was also linked to a duplicate account — liberating the email address from the duplicate account and associating it with the main account helped resolve the problem.
